questions.question: A customer asks whether you use AI to process their data. You paste their email address and purchase history into ChatGPT to draft a personalised marketing email. Under the Privacy Act 2020, which statement is most accurate?

answers.text: The Privacy Act doesn't apply because ChatGPT is a third-party tool, not your system
answers.feedback: The Privacy Act 2020 applies to all businesses that handle personal information, regardless of size. When you use an AI tool to process customer data, you remain responsible under the 13 Information Privacy Principles — including how the tool stores, uses, and discloses that data.

answers.text: You are responsible for how the customer's personal information is handled, including by AI tools you use
answers.feedback: The Privacy Act 2020 applies to all businesses that handle personal information, regardless of size. When you use an AI tool to process customer data, you remain responsible under the 13 Information Privacy Principles — including how the tool stores, uses, and discloses that data.

answers.text: As long as the customer has previously bought from you, you can use their data in any AI tool
answers.feedback: The Privacy Act 2020 applies to all businesses that handle personal information, regardless of size. When you use an AI tool to process customer data, you remain responsible under the 13 Information Privacy Principles — including how the tool stores, uses, and discloses that data.

answers.text: The Privacy Act only applies to businesses with more than 20 employees
answers.feedback: The Privacy Act 2020 applies to all businesses that handle personal information, regardless of size. When you use an AI tool to process customer data, you remain responsible under the 13 Information Privacy Principles — including how the tool stores, uses, and discloses that data.

questions.question: Your AI marketing tool generates the claim 'We offer the lowest prices in Canterbury.' What should you do before publishing this?

answers.text: Publish it — the AI generated it based on data, so it must be accurate
answers.feedback: Under the Fair Trading Act, you must have reasonable grounds for any claims your business makes. 'The AI wrote it' is not a defence. With maximum fines increasing to $5M from 2026, fact-checking AI-generated marketing claims is essential. Even vague claims like 'competitive' could require substantiation if challenged.

answers.text: Add a disclaimer saying 'AI-generated claim, not verified'
answers.feedback: Under the Fair Trading Act, you must have reasonable grounds for any claims your business makes. 'The AI wrote it' is not a defence. With maximum fines increasing to $5M from 2026, fact-checking AI-generated marketing claims is essential. Even vague claims like 'competitive' could require substantiation if challenged.

answers.text: Verify the claim with evidence, or change it to something you can substantiate
answers.feedback: Under the Fair Trading Act, you must have reasonable grounds for any claims your business makes. 'The AI wrote it' is not a defence. With maximum fines increasing to $5M from 2026, fact-checking AI-generated marketing claims is essential. Even vague claims like 'competitive' could require substantiation if challenged.

answers.text: Remove the word 'lowest' and replace it with 'competitive' — no evidence needed for vague terms
answers.feedback: Under the Fair Trading Act, you must have reasonable grounds for any claims your business makes. 'The AI wrote it' is not a defence. With maximum fines increasing to $5M from 2026, fact-checking AI-generated marketing claims is essential. Even vague claims like 'competitive' could require substantiation if challenged.

questions.question: You're choosing an AI tool for your business. Which of the following are important considerations under IPP 12 (cross-border data transfers)? Select all that apply.

answers.text: Where the AI tool processes and stores data
answers.feedback: IPP 12 requires that personal information sent overseas is protected by comparable privacy standards. Key considerations include where data is processed, what protections are in place, whether business accounts offer stronger terms than free tiers, and whether NZ-hosted alternatives exist (such as the AWS NZ region launched in 2025).

answers.text: Whether the tool has data protection agreements comparable to NZ standards
answers.feedback: IPP 12 requires that personal information sent overseas is protected by comparable privacy standards. Key considerations include where data is processed, what protections are in place, whether business accounts offer stronger terms than free tiers, and whether NZ-hosted alternatives exist (such as the AWS NZ region launched in 2025).

answers.text: The colour scheme and branding of the AI tool
answers.feedback: IPP 12 requires that personal information sent overseas is protected by comparable privacy standards. Key considerations include where data is processed, what protections are in place, whether business accounts offer stronger terms than free tiers, and whether NZ-hosted alternatives exist (such as the AWS NZ region launched in 2025).

answers.text: Whether business accounts offer stronger privacy protections than free tiers
answers.feedback: IPP 12 requires that personal information sent overseas is protected by comparable privacy standards. Key considerations include where data is processed, what protections are in place, whether business accounts offer stronger terms than free tiers, and whether NZ-hosted alternatives exist (such as the AWS NZ region launched in 2025).

answers.text: Whether NZ-hosted options are available
answers.feedback: IPP 12 requires that personal information sent overseas is protected by comparable privacy standards. Key considerations include where data is processed, what protections are in place, whether business accounts offer stronger terms than free tiers, and whether NZ-hosted alternatives exist (such as the AWS NZ region launched in 2025).

questions.question: Under NZ copyright law, who owns the copyright in content generated by an AI tool?

answers.text: The AI tool's developer always owns the copyright
answers.feedback: NZ is one of few countries that does protect copyright in computer-generated works. Under the Copyright Act 1994, copyright belongs to the person who made the 'arrangements necessary for the creation of the work.' The more active human involvement (detailed prompts, editing, creative decisions), the stronger the claim. However, the AI tool's terms of service may also affect IP ownership.

answers.text: Nobody — AI-generated content cannot be copyrighted
answers.feedback: NZ is one of few countries that does protect copyright in computer-generated works. Under the Copyright Act 1994, copyright belongs to the person who made the 'arrangements necessary for the creation of the work.' The more active human involvement (detailed prompts, editing, creative decisions), the stronger the claim. However, the AI tool's terms of service may also affect IP ownership.

answers.text: The person who made the arrangements necessary for its creation, subject to the tool's terms of service
answers.feedback: NZ is one of few countries that does protect copyright in computer-generated works. Under the Copyright Act 1994, copyright belongs to the person who made the 'arrangements necessary for the creation of the work.' The more active human involvement (detailed prompts, editing, creative decisions), the stronger the claim. However, the AI tool's terms of service may also affect IP ownership.

answers.text: Copyright is automatically shared equally between the user and the AI company
answers.feedback: NZ is one of few countries that does protect copyright in computer-generated works. Under the Copyright Act 1994, copyright belongs to the person who made the 'arrangements necessary for the creation of the work.' The more active human involvement (detailed prompts, editing, creative decisions), the stronger the claim. However, the AI tool's terms of service may also affect IP ownership.

questions.question: Which of Te Mana Raraunga's Maori Data Sovereignty principles should a business consider when using AI to process data about Maori communities? Select all that apply.

answers.text: Rangatiratanga — Maori have the right to govern how data about them is used
answers.feedback: All three principles apply. Te Mana Raraunga's principles are relevant to any organisation — not just government — that handles data about Maori. Rangatiratanga recognises Maori authority over their data, Kaitiakitanga calls for responsible guardianship, and Manaakitanga requires that data use provides reciprocal benefit. Businesses serving Maori communities should consider all six principles.

answers.text: Kaitiakitanga — those holding the data have a responsibility to protect and care for it
answers.feedback: All three principles apply. Te Mana Raraunga's principles are relevant to any organisation — not just government — that handles data about Maori. Rangatiratanga recognises Maori authority over their data, Kaitiakitanga calls for responsible guardianship, and Manaakitanga requires that data use provides reciprocal benefit. Businesses serving Maori communities should consider all six principles.

answers.text: Manaakitanga — data use should provide benefit back to Maori communities
answers.feedback: All three principles apply. Te Mana Raraunga's principles are relevant to any organisation — not just government — that handles data about Maori. Rangatiratanga recognises Maori authority over their data, Kaitiakitanga calls for responsible guardianship, and Manaakitanga requires that data use provides reciprocal benefit. Businesses serving Maori communities should consider all six principles.

answers.text: None of these — data sovereignty only applies to government agencies
answers.feedback: All three principles apply. Te Mana Raraunga's principles are relevant to any organisation — not just government — that handles data about Maori. Rangatiratanga recognises Maori authority over their data, Kaitiakitanga calls for responsible guardianship, and Manaakitanga requires that data use provides reciprocal benefit. Businesses serving Maori communities should consider all six principles.

questions.question: Your business uses fingerprint scanners for staff time tracking. Under the Biometric Processing Privacy Code 2025, what is your compliance deadline?

answers.text: The Code doesn't apply to fingerprint scanners, only facial recognition
answers.feedback: The Biometric Processing Privacy Code 2025 came into force on 3 November 2025, with a grace period for compliance until 3 August 2026. It covers all biometric data, including fingerprints, facial recognition, voiceprints, iris scans, and gait analysis — not just facial recognition.

answers.text: 3 August 2026
answers.feedback: The Biometric Processing Privacy Code 2025 came into force on 3 November 2025, with a grace period for compliance until 3 August 2026. It covers all biometric data, including fingerprints, facial recognition, voiceprints, iris scans, and gait analysis — not just facial recognition.

answers.text: Compliance was required immediately when the Code came into force in November 2025
answers.feedback: The Biometric Processing Privacy Code 2025 came into force on 3 November 2025, with a grace period for compliance until 3 August 2026. It covers all biometric data, including fingerprints, facial recognition, voiceprints, iris scans, and gait analysis — not just facial recognition.

answers.text: There is no deadline — the Code is voluntary
answers.feedback: The Biometric Processing Privacy Code 2025 came into force on 3 November 2025, with a grace period for compliance until 3 August 2026. It covers all biometric data, including fingerprints, facial recognition, voiceprints, iris scans, and gait analysis — not just facial recognition.

questions.question: You're creating an AI workplace policy for your small business. Which of the following should be included? Select all that apply.

answers.text: A list of approved AI tools
answers.feedback: An effective AI policy includes approved tools, data handling rules, quality check requirements, and a regular review cycle. Banning AI entirely is not practical — 52% of NZ businesses already identify shadow AI as a problem, which means staff will use AI regardless. A well-designed policy empowers responsible use rather than driving it underground.

answers.text: Clear rules about what data can and cannot be entered into AI tools
answers.feedback: An effective AI policy includes approved tools, data handling rules, quality check requirements, and a regular review cycle. Banning AI entirely is not practical — 52% of NZ businesses already identify shadow AI as a problem, which means staff will use AI regardless. A well-designed policy empowers responsible use rather than driving it underground.

answers.text: A requirement that all AI-generated content is reviewed by a human before use
answers.feedback: An effective AI policy includes approved tools, data handling rules, quality check requirements, and a regular review cycle. Banning AI entirely is not practical — 52% of NZ businesses already identify shadow AI as a problem, which means staff will use AI regardless. A well-designed policy empowers responsible use rather than driving it underground.

answers.text: A ban on all AI use to eliminate risk
answers.feedback: An effective AI policy includes approved tools, data handling rules, quality check requirements, and a regular review cycle. Banning AI entirely is not practical — 52% of NZ businesses already identify shadow AI as a problem, which means staff will use AI regardless. A well-designed policy empowers responsible use rather than driving it underground.

answers.text: A review schedule to keep the policy current
answers.feedback: An effective AI policy includes approved tools, data handling rules, quality check requirements, and a regular review cycle. questions.question: A staff member at your business accidentally pastes a client's confidential financial records into a free AI chatbot. Which insurance type would most likely cover the resulting data breach costs?

answers.text: General liability insurance
answers.feedback: Cyber liability insurance is designed to cover data breach costs, including notification, forensic investigation, legal fees, and business interruption. With 43% of business leaders identifying accidental employee data exposure via AI as their biggest cyber risk, cyber liability insurance is increasingly important for businesses using AI tools. Professional indemnity insurance may also apply if the breach involves client professional services.

answers.text: Cyber liability insurance
answers.feedback: Cyber liability insurance is designed to cover data breach costs, including notification, forensic investigation, legal fees, and business interruption. With 43% of business leaders identifying accidental employee data exposure via AI as their biggest cyber risk, cyber liability insurance is increasingly important for businesses using AI tools. Professional indemnity insurance may also apply if the breach involves client professional services.

answers.text: Contents insurance
answers.feedback: Cyber liability insurance is designed to cover data breach costs, including notification, forensic investigation, legal fees, and business interruption. answers.text: Public liability insurance
